Navodaya Circuits Limited — India Privacy and UPSI Handling Notice for investors, employees, vendors, and website users under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and SEBI PIT Regulations.
We collect and process names, contact details, employment and shareholding data, and identifiers such as PAN (masked), Aadhaar (tokenised; last four only), and GSTIN (where mandated), strictly for KYC, payroll, tax, compliance, and exchange disclosure purposes with consent or other lawful grounds.
For children (under 18), we only process data with verifiable consent of a parent or lawful guardian and provide age-appropriate notices.
Submit access/correction/erasure or grievance requests to privacy@navodayacircuits.in or by post to Data Protection Office, Navodaya Circuits Limited, Plot 88, Synergy Tech Park, Sector 21, New Vasudev Nagar, Pune, Maharashtra; we respond within reasonable time under the DPDPA.
Designated Grievance Officer and DPO: Mr. Raghav Venkataraman (dpo@navodayacircuits.in); escalation to the Board Governance Committee is available for unresolved grievances.
Retention: employee and investor records are kept for legal limitation periods; structured digital database entries (UPSI recipients, event logs) are retained for at least 8 years or until completion of SEBI proceedings, whichever is later; Aadhaar is stored as an irreversible token separate from primary records.
UPSI means any information relating to Navodaya Circuits Limited that is not generally available and which, upon becoming generally available, is likely to materially affect our securities’ price; “generally available” includes disclosures on the NSE/BSE websites and our Investor Relations page.
Trading Window is closed from the end of each quarter until 48 hours after financial results become generally available; financial results are disclosed to the stock exchanges per applicable SEBI LODR timelines following Board approval.
Selective disclosure is prohibited; if any investor/analyst interaction risks inadvertent UPSI, we will first or simultaneously disseminate the same to NSE and BSE and publish on our website in accordance with fair disclosure principles.
As publicly announced in our BSE filing dated 14 April 2025 regarding a planned Unit‑3 maintenance outage, that information is generally available and not UPSI; by contrast, unannounced draft capacity expansion terms remain UPSI until disclosed.
Materiality is assessed on an issuer‑relative basis: for example, a pilot supplier change for a single production line at our Hyderabad facility is not material until a binding LOI is executed or revenue/cost impact thresholds under our policy are met.
Tea will be served at the AGM registration desk; catering arrangements and a vendor’s shift to a different courier partner are non‑issuer operational updates and do not constitute UPSI.
Cross‑border transfers (e.g., to a payroll processor in Singapore) occur under contractual safeguards consistent with the DPDPA and any Central Government notifications; primary servers are located in India, and Aadhaar tokens are not transferred.
To process DSARs securely, we may request limited verification (e.g., last four digits of PAN, masked Aadhaar token, or registered folio reference), and we will provide reasons for denial where permitted by law (e.g., legal privilege, regulatory hold).
Press releases dated 31 March 2025 uploaded to the NSE electronic platform and our IR page are already in the public domain and should not be treated as UPSI or confidential.
For consent withdrawal, email consent@navodayacircuits.in; withdrawal will not affect past processing and may limit services (e.g., dividend payout compliance checks) where statutory processing is required.
